paper.title: Bile acid-containing lipid nanoparticles enhance extrahepatic mRNA delivery

formulations.targeting_details: Cholesterol fully replaced with cholic acid (CA). CA-100 = 100% cholic acid replacement. Four-component LNP with bile acid instead of cholesterol.
formulations.formulation_notes: CA-100 is highly spleen-tropic. Cholic acid, chenodeoxycholic acid, deoxycholic acid, and lithocholic acid all tested at various replacement ratios. Cholic acid at 100% replacement (CA-100) = best spleen tropism. Generalizable: C12-200 with cholic acid also shifted liver → spleen. LNPs displayed polygonal morphology by cryo-EM.

results.liver_metric: Spleen-tropic; liver delivery reduced vs standard cholesterol LNP
results.liver_notes: CA-100 shifted delivery from liver to spleen. Mechanism unclear — could be differential protein corona or morphology-driven (polygonal shape). Also demonstrated reduced liver delivery in vitro.

safety.tolerability_notes: Bile acids are naturally occurring — potentially good tolerability profile.

experiments.notes: Spleen-tropic. BM NOT measured. In vitro: reduced liver cell delivery, improved delivery to T cells, B cells, epithelial cells. Generalizable across two ionizable lipids (C14-4 and C12-200). Polygonal LNP morphology is unusual and may contribute to tropism.

key_findings_for_project: Bile acid (cholic acid) fully replaces cholesterol for liver de-targeting — another cholesterol replacement strategy
Converges with Su/Liu 2024 finding: cholesterol = liver sink
Generalizable across C14-4 and C12-200 ionizable lipids
Polygonal LNP morphology by cryo-EM — novel shape may affect biodistribution
In vitro: improved delivery to T cells, B cells, epithelial cells — immune cell tropism
BM NOT measured — another study where BM is absent from organ panel
Mitchell lab (UPenn) — same lab as Palanki CD45-targeted in utero paper

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: Different approach. Bile acid replacement could complement antibody targeting.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_shi: Could combine bile acid LNP with CD117 targeting for dual liver de-targeting.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_kim: Different cholesterol strategy. Kim's formulations contain standard cholesterol.
comparison_to_existing_data.new_pareto_points: No BM data. Spleen-tropic, not BM-tropic.
comparison_to_existing_data.therapeutic_window_update: Third independent confirmation that cholesterol replacement de-targets liver (after glycolipid/Gentry and cholesterol removal/Su).
comparison_to_existing_data.gap_formulation_relevance: Medium. C14-4 and cholic acid are known compounds. The bile acid replacement is a novel feature not in your current matrix. Could encode as cholesterol_analog = cholic_acid.

limitations_and_caveats: Mechanism for spleen tropism unclear (protein corona vs morphology)
Polygonal morphology may be unstable for clinical development
Only reporter readout (luciferase), no editing cargo
Bile acids have their own biological activity (FXR agonism) — potential confound
